steps.title: Identify the key context
steps.content: The sentence says Edwards "has a ___ painting portraits" and the next sentence calls those works "characteristic" — meaning he is known for them. The blank must convey being recognized or known for something.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'reputation for': fits precisely — having a reputation for painting portraits means being known or recognized for it, matching "characteristic works." ✓
  • Choice B — 'requirement for': reverses the logic — a requirement is an obligation imposed on someone, not recognition earned; nothing in the passage suggests Edwards is obligated to paint portraits. ✗
  • Choice C — 'question about': contradicts the tone — the passage presents the portraits positively as "esteemed," so questioning them makes no sense. ✗
  • Choice D — 'complaint about': contradicts the tone — "esteemed" portraits are praised, not complained about. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
